name: Joseph W-Mary C Wood Sch Bus
recent_news: Maryland corporation incorporated August 11, 2009; in good standing as of recent state records; Mary C. Wood is registered agent. No M&A or significant operational news located.
fit_notes: Tiny husband-and-wife MD school bus contractor in St. Mary's County area — consistent with the 250+ contractor-owned bus model in the county. Too small to be a stand-alone tuck-in but could fit a roll-up of small Southern Maryland contractors; succession-aged owners are a plus.

name: Valley Park School Bus Inc
districts_or_customers: Likely South Delta School District or Sharkey-Issaquena Academy (Rolling Fork/Valley Park area)
recent_news: No web footprint, news, or M&A activity located. South Delta SD operates its own fleet per FMCSA, suggesting Valley Park School Bus may serve private/parochial routes or be a contracted owner-operator entity.
fit_notes: Very small Delta-region MS operator with minimal digital footprint. MS is not a Summit priority state and scale is sub-tuck-in size; low priority unless part of a wider Delta consolidation play.

name: Queen City Transportation
owner_or_parent: National Express LLC (Durham School Services / Summit School Services platform)
principals: Aaron Haid (former owner; repurchased motor-coach division 2021)
recent_news: Acquired by National Express LLC at the end of 2017 (STB approval Nov 2017); originally Klug Bus Service (1959), purchased and rebranded by Aaron Haid in 2014. Haid repurchased ONLY the motor-coach division in Jan 2021; the school-bus operations remained with National Express/Durham.
fit_notes: NOT A TARGET — school-bus side is already part of the National Express/Durham platform (i.e., Summit). The 1053 Ebenezer Rd address corresponds to the legacy school-bus yard absorbed by National Express. The motor-coach division (separate website) is independent again under Haid but is a charter business, not a Summit fit. Exclude from buyer list.

name: Bluff Country Bus-Plainview
principals: Ken Grisim (Doug's father, prior bus operator in Northfield MN since 1969)
recent_news: Doug & Cara Grisim founded Bluff Country in 1995 by acquiring 8 buses from Raleigh & MaryAnn Tiedeman in Lake City; grown organically to 54 buses across 3 SE-MN districts. Doug is past President and current Board member of the Minnesota School Bus Operators Association.
fit_notes: Strong tuck-in candidate: classic 2nd-generation family-owned MN contractor, ~54 buses across 3 stable districts, owner is industry-association alumnus (suggests trade-network credibility). Founders in business 30+ years implies succession runway. Best contact angle is Doug Grisim directly via MSBOA network.

name: R & R Big Bus
recent_news: No web footprint, website, or news located. (512) 759-3900 maps to the Liberty Hill / Burnet / Lago Vista exchange in central TX; no confirmation as an active school-bus contractor. Possible owner-operator or dormant entity. Not the same as R&R Limousine & Bus in Austin.
fit_notes: Insufficient information to recommend. Likely sub-scale owner-operator in Texas Hill Country if real; verify with TxDOT carrier records and a phone call before any outreach. Low priority pending data.
